name: Tiled
description: Free, open-source 2D map editor for tile-based games. Exports TMX maps readable by every major 2D game engine—widely used in RPGs, platformers, and strategy games.

seo.title: Tiled — Game Development Tools
seo.meta_description: Tiled: Free, open-source 2D map editor for tile-based games. Exports TMX maps readable by every major 2D game engine—widely used in RPGs, platformers, and strat

when_to_use: RPG world map built as multi-layer Tiled TMX project
Platformer level exported as JSON and loaded into Phaser game
Entity metadata attached to map objects and read in engine
Tileset collision masks defined in Tiled and imported to Unity
Open-source level editor integrated into Godot 2D project

pros: Stable, battle-tested since 2008 with huge ecosystem
TMX format readable by almost every 2D engine
Donation-based, truly open source under GPL
Isometric and hex grid support out of the box
cons: UI feels dated compared to LDtk
Auto-tile rules less polished than LDtks
Workflow becomes tedious on very large worlds
review.verdict: The safe, broadly-compatible 2D level editor; LDtk feels more modern, but Tileds ecosystem advantage remains real.
review.best_for: Isometric, hex, or orthogonal 2D games needing broad engine compatibility
review.not_for: Teams wanting the most modern UX — LDtk has the edge there
